transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports right hip pain since February 2020.
*   The patient reports pain is worse with movement.
*   The patient reports pain in the groin area.
*   The patient reports pain when pivoting.
*   The patient reports pain ranges from 2 to 7 out of 10.
*   The patient denies fever or chills.
*   The patient denies tingling or numbness.
*   The patient denies bowel or bladder problems.
*   The patient reports pain when standing and taking steps.
*   The patient reports decreased range of motion with exertion.
*   The patient reports pain with resisted right hip flexion.
*   The patient reports pain with right hip flection.
*   The patient reports pain when lifting the knee.
*   The patient reports pain when squeezing knees together.
*   The patient reports pain when kicking leg out straight.
*   The patient reports pain when leaning back.
*   The clinician suspects right hip degenerative joint disease.
*   The clinician plans a low-impact exercise program.
*   The clinician plans the use of NSAIDs.
*   The clinician plans the use of a cane.
*   The clinician plans a cortisone injection into the right hip joint.
*   The clinician explained the risks of injection, including needles, sterile and covid.
